items.title: The Best Dividend ETF to Buy: SCHD Pays a High Yield While VIG Focuses on Dividend Growth
items.author: Neha Chamaria
items.description: The article compares two leading dividend ETFs: Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) and Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG). SCHD offers a higher dividend yield of 3.8% but lower total returns, while VIG provides broader diversification with 338 holdings and stronger long-term growth potential through dividend reinvestment. Both are low-cost options suitable for passive income generation, with choice depending on whether investors prioritize current income or long-term growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SCHD is highlighted for its significantly higher dividend yield of 3.8% and lower volatility (beta 0.73), making it attractive for income-focused investors seeking stable, reliable dividend payments from high-quality dividend payers.

VIG is praised for superior long-term total returns (14.9% 1-yr return vs 6% for SCHD), broader diversification with 338 holdings, and focus on dividend growth stocks with 10+ years of consecutive dividend increases, demonstrating stronger growth potential when dividends are reinvested.
